rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NISSIM LANYADOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/13/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing has been filed. (The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-020, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (5) For a first accounting, original statements immediately preceding the date the conservator was appointed must be filed. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).] (6) The I&A filed on 7/30/24 shows assets that should have been appraised by a probate referee on Attachment 1, which is just for cash or cash equivalents. (7) The Summary of Account shows cash, but none was inventoried. (8) The Summary of Account also does not balance. (9) The disbursements schedule should have four columns showing the date, payee, description, and amount. (10) Does the allegation in paragraph 5 mean that the conservator did not retain cash in interest-bearing accounts? (11) The prayer includes relief not disclosed or requested in the petition (e.g., approving fees, confirming bond). (12) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. A proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (an amended account, proof of service of notice of the amended account, bank statements, and declaration regarding the supporting bank statement(s), etc.) must be received by the assigned examiner by 9/29/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (The successor conservator was appointed on 3/7/24, not 4/10/24. She claimed her Letters on 4/10/24, not 4/4/24.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAXINE GRODJINSKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/25/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). 2) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the number of shares of securities and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary (LRSF 14.35.I.a). 3) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. The order must state specifically within the body of the order how the estate is to be distributed, as set forth in the petition. (LRSF 14.35.I.6.b). Please supplement the petition and provide courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 08/18/2025 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JASON CHOI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/13/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) No ex parte petition has been filed "promptly" to increase bond, as alleged in paragraph 8 of the petition. (Does bond need to be increased?) (3) No allowance or rejection of the creditor's claim has been filed. [Probate Code Section 9250(b).] (4) Accountings in decedents' estates is based on the I&A (carry value), not the cost basis of Decedent's holdings. (5) How much is "this sum" that is expected to be received from the state's unclaimed property division? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/29/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT YEARGIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/25/25. The following issues to be cleared or addressed by the petitioner's verified supplement before 8/18/2025: (1) Need clarification regarding the date of death, along with a copy of the death certificate or equivalent proof that indicates the date. The records show conflicting dates: Petition for Probate (7/5/24), Order for Probate (7/9/24), Inventory and Appraisal (7/15/24), and the instant petition (7/9/24). (2) As the petition reports losses of the sale, the statutory fee base computation needs to reflect the losses of $60K from the sale of the property. Prob. Code sec. 10810(b). CRC 7.750(b). (3) Petition to ascertain whether the funds being deposited have sufficient FDIC coverage of $250,000, as the current balance exceeds this amount.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL TREFZGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 5/13/2020. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. (The Court will waive defective notice to George "Bud" Reneau for this hearing only, based on the petitioner's allegation that her attorney spoke with the interested party. Serving notice on 7/29/25 for a hearing on 8/4/25 is insufficient notice. It would have been better to have had him waive notice for this hearing.) Status hearing will be set for 2/8/27. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date.
